heading.purpose: Section and card titles; the most prominent text on most screens.

body.purpose: Default reading and data size; prose capped 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Nav labels, badges, metadata, small UI text.

shadows.name: none
shadows.purpose: System is flat by default. No drop-shadow vocabulary.

shadows.name: card-hairline
shadows.purpose: The only resting separation on cards: a 1px inset-style ring, not a shadow.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default for hover/focus/color state changes; conveys state, not choreography.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default action button; the one high-contrast commitment on a quiet screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 10px; background: oklch(0.205 0 0); color: oklch(1 0 0); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out, transform 100ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.205 0 0 / 0.8); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.708 0 0); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.708 0 0 / 0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action; hairline border on the paper surface.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0 0); border-radius: 10px; background: oklch(1 0 0); color: oklch(0.145 0 0); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.97 0 0); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.708 0 0); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.708 0 0 / 0.5); }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Delete / destructive action as a tinted fill, never a solid red slab.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 10px; background: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325 / 0.1); color: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { background: oklch(0.577 0.245 27.325 / 0.2); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat container separated by a hairline ring, not a shadow.
components.html:
Bollo auto
Scade il 30 settembre · € 212,00
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; padding: 16px; border-radius: 14px; background: oklch(1 0 0); color: oklch(0.145 0 0); box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px oklch(0.145 0 0 / 0.1); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.375; } .ds-card-desc { font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.556 0 0); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Hairline field with a focus ring matching the button focus language.
components.css: .ds-input { height: 32px; width: 100%; padding: 4px 10px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0 0); border-radius: 10px; background: transparent; color: oklch(0.145 0 0); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: border-color 150ms ease-out, box-shadow 150ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.556 0 0); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.708 0 0); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.708 0 0 / 0.5); }

components.name: Due-Soon Deadline Row
components.description: Signature pattern: a deadline row on a plain hairline card. Due-soon is signalled by warm-amber meta text (the --warning token, #b45309) and a category-tinted chip; overdue turns the meta red. Never a colored side-stripe or a full amber border.
components.html: RevisioneRevisione Panda23/11/2026 · tra 5 giorni · Panda di Giulia66,00 €
components.css: .ds-deadline { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; justify-content: space-between; gap: 12px; padding: 12px 14px; border-radius: 14px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.921 0.007 75); background: oklch(0.994 0.005 75); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-deadline-body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; min-width: 0; } .ds-chip { align-self: flex-start; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.145 0 0); background: color-mix(in oklch, #eb6834 15%, transparent); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklch, #eb6834 30%, transparent); } .ds-deadline-title { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.145 0 0); } .ds-deadline-meta { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #b45309; } .ds-deadline-amount { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.145 0 0); }

components.name: Bottom Tab Nav Item
components.description: Mobile bottom-bar nav item: icon over a small label; active is ink, inactive is muted.
components.html: HomeScadenze
components.css: .ds-tabbar { display: flex; justify-content: space-around; border-top: 1px solid oklch(0.922 0 0); background: oklch(1 0 0); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-tab { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 4px; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.75rem; color: oklch(0.556 0 0); text-decoration: none; cursor: pointer; } .ds-tab:hover { color: oklch(0.145 0 0); } .ds-tab--active { color: oklch(0.205 0 0); }

narrative.overview: FamilySherpa is a quiet, trustworthy record of a household's obligations, kept so no one has to keep them in their head. The visual system exists to make that offloading feel like relief, not like another dashboard to manage. Surfaces are warm paper-plain with ink-black type and hairline borders; the neutral ramp carries only a sub-perceptual warm tint (~0.006 chroma) for warmth, so when a nameable color appears it still means something. Warmth is carried by tone, copy, and generous touch targets, never by loud decoration. The system is deliberately restrained so foresight can lead: every screen surfaces what's coming above what already happened.
keyCharacteristics: Warm near-neutral surfaces (a whisper of warm chroma, not chroma-0); nameable color reserved for meaning (warnings, errors, category, data).
Single typeface (Inter) across the whole product; no display/body split.
Flat by default: depth from hairline rings and tonal fills, not shadows.
Mobile-first: a bottom tab bar on phones, a left rail on desktop.
Warm without childish; reassurance from tone and space, never mascots or toy shapes.

rules.name: The Meaning-Only Color Rule
rules.body: The resting interface is near chroma 0: surfaces carry only a sub-perceptual warm tint (~0.006 chroma) and ink text stays chroma-0. Every nameable hue must carry information: amber = attention soon, red = overdue/error, a category hue = a specific deadline category (on its chip and in charts alike). Color for decoration is forbidden.

rules.name: The One Navy Rule
rules.body: #0f172a lives in the app manifest and status bar and nowhere else. It is the frame around the window, never paint inside it.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: Inter does all the work. Adding a second typeface is prohibited; warmth comes from tone and spacing.

rules.name: The No-Display Rule
rules.body: No fluid clamp() headings, no oversized hero type. A heading that grows to fill the viewport is shouting, which this system never does.

rules.name: The Hairline-Not-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Separation is drawn with a 1px ring or border and a tonal fill, never a box-shadow. A drop shadow reads as a foreign SaaS card.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat until the user touches them. Elevation-like feedback is a response to state, not a resting decoration.

dos: Do keep resting surfaces at near chroma 0 (only the sub-perceptual warm tint); introduce a nameable hue only when it names something (amber = due soon, red = overdue, a fixed category hue = a category on its chip or chart).
Do draw separation with a 1px ring/border plus a tonal fill (bg-muted/50), never a drop shadow.
Do set every type element in Inter; carry hierarchy with size and weight, not a second face.
Do lead each screen with foresight above logs of what already happened.
Do keep the amber and red voices gentle (tinted fills, hairline borders); reassure, don't alarm.
Do assign chart colors in the fixed category order from src/lib/deadline-labels.ts; never cycle hues per render.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion and keep transitions at 150-250ms conveying state only.
donts: Don't build a cold government/bank portal: no dense, sterile, form-heavy layouts.
Don't turn warm into childish: no cartoon mascots, pastel bubbles, or toy-rounded shapes.
Don't ship a loud SaaS dashboard: no gradient hero-metrics, glassmorphism, neon accents, or gradient text.
Don't become a cluttered do-everything app: one clear primary task per screen.
Don't use #0f172a (brand navy) as a UI accent or surface; it is app chrome only.
Don't add box-shadows, colored side-stripe borders, or fluid clamp() hero type.
Don't use muted gray for primary reading text; body copy stays at ink contrast.
